name: ProGuides
description: Subscription and marketplace for game coaching and on-demand courses taught by pro players, targeting competitive and improving gamers.

details.targetMarket: Individual competitive gamers (casual-to-semi-pro) seeking skill improvement via courses, 1:1 coaching, and ‘play with the pros’ sessions

items: On-demand video courses and lessons created with pro players
Masterclasses and bootcamps across titles (LoL, CSGO, Fortnite, etc.)
Roster of professional coaches available for 1:1 sessions
Play-with-the-pros / scrim sessions where users can schedule matches with pros
Pro membership (marketing price $7.99/mo) that unlocks course library and discounts
Integration/merger with Aimlab+ to combine aim training and ProGuides membership
Desktop app (Overwolf) with coach roster, course catalogue and AI chatbot
Data-driven progress trackers mentioned in company descriptions

missingFeatures: Opaque subscription billing: marketed as $7.99/mo but commonly sold as an annual charge by default (heavy fine-print), which creates buyer confusion and refund disputes
Coaching purchase friction: 1:1 sessions are bought via 'PG Points' rather than transparent per-session pricing, preventing easy price comparison and driving additional microtransactions
No built-in recurring coaching bookings or subscription-for-coaching flow (weekly recurring sessions) — coaches are booked ad-hoc per session, increasing friction for users who want regular coaching
Limited buyer protection and refund transparency for paid coaching sessions — multiple user reports of difficulty getting refunds or resolving billing disputes
No obvious public B2B/team or API offering for esports organizations or coaching programs (limits expansion into team/enterprise workflows)

pricingPlans.name: Free
pricingPlans.note: Limited access to free guides and some coach listings; most premium courses and discounts reserved for paid members

pricingPlans.name: Pro (marketing)
pricingPlans.note: Marketed monthly price; historically sold as an annual subscription by default — includes access to course library, Aimlabs+ bundling benefits, and coaching discounts

pricingPlans.name: Pro (monthly billed)
pricingPlans.note: Reported monthly billing price (varies by promotion); coaching sessions are purchased separately via PG Points

pricingEvidence.label: Forbes (reporting pricing)
pricingEvidence.claim: Article states ProGuides offered a subscription at $7.99 per month when reporting on company funding.

pricingEvidence.label: ProGuides - Subscriptions page
pricingEvidence.claim: Official subscriptions/settings page where membership tiers and billing options are presented.

pricingEvidence.label: Dot Esports (product description)
pricingEvidence.claim: Describes ProGuides as offering a $7.99-per-month subscription and on-demand coaching.

companyEvidence.label: BusinessWire (acquisition press release)
companyEvidence.claim: Statespace acquired ProGuides in November 2021; press release notes ProGuides was founded in 2015.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase (company profile & funding rounds)
companyEvidence.claim: Lists ProGuides funding rounds including a $5.5M seed and a $1.9M pre-seed, describing the company as an on-demand coaching and courses platform.

companyEvidence.label: PitchBook (company snapshot)
companyEvidence.claim: Company profile showing founding year (2015), headquarters in Los Angeles, and an estimated employee count (~79).

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot (user reviews)
reviewEvidence.claim: Multiple user reviews reporting confusion over advertised monthly pricing vs annual billing and complaints about refund/billing experiences.

reviewEvidence.label: EsportsBettingSite (critique of billing/PG Points)
reviewEvidence.claim: Article criticizes ProGuides for marketing $7.99/mo but billing annually and for requiring PG Points for coaching purchases, creating perceived predatory UX.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit user threads
reviewEvidence.claim: Community posts recount negative experiences around subscription model, billing, and perceived poor value.

featureEvidence.label: ProGuides - Guides & Courses
featureEvidence.claim: Official course catalog and guides showing on-demand courses by pro players across multiple titles.

featureEvidence.label: ProGuides blog: Aimlabs+ and ProGuides memberships combining
featureEvidence.claim: Announcement that Aimlabs+ and ProGuides memberships are being combined to give members access to Aim Lab tools and ProGuides content.

featureEvidence.label: Overwolf - The ProGuides App
featureEvidence.claim: Overwolf app listing describes features like AI chatbot, coach roster, and course catalogue available in the desktop app.

strengths: High-profile pro content: Courses and masterclasses from well-known pro players provide credibility and discoverability (e.g., s1mple, Imaqtpie referenced in press).
Aim Lab / Statespace integration: Acquisition by Statespace and Aimlab+ bundling expands technical training (aim analytics) and gives product differentiation vs pure-marketplace rivals.
Affordable entry price: Low advertised entry price point (marketed at $7.99/mo) makes the product accessible to a large consumer base.
Breadth of content and marketplace: Combination of on-demand courses, live 1:1 coaching, and play-with-pro sessions addresses multiple learner preferences and monetization channels.
Desktop & platform integrations: Presence on Overwolf and Aim Lab integration increases in-game or desktop discoverability.
weaknesses: Billing and transparency issues: Repeated complaints about annual default billing and unclear messaging damage trust and increase refund friction (Trustpilot/Reddit).
Opaque coaching commerce (PG Points): Using points to buy coaching (rather than clear per-session pricing) creates friction and obscures real cost-to-serve comparisons between coaches.
Mixed user reviews: While many praise coaches, there is a notable volume of complaints focused on billing and perceived value that harm conversion for skeptical buyers.
Marketplace quality control & guarantees: Limited visible buyer protections or standardized coach verification beyond listings and ratings makes high-ticket purchases riskier.
Limited B2B / team features: No clear public offering for teams, organizations, or APIs to integrate ProGuides coaching into enterprise or club workflows, constraining expansion.
